name: socratic-codex
description: Goal-lifecycle control for coding agents: bind intent to a persisted goal contract, gate risky changes, restore the contract after compaction, and block unverified completion claims with behavioral evidence checks.

interface.shortDescription: Prevent goal drift in long tasks.
interface.longDescription: Socratic Codex protects user-owned intent from discovery through acceptance. It persists the goal contract to .socratic/contract.md, restores it after compaction or resume, re-anchors on brake or drift signals, asks only action-changing user-owned questions, and blocks completion claims until behavioral evidence (verification commands run, or a fresh Verification record in the contract) matches done criteria. Use it for /goal drafting and steering, sustained solution-shaped work, risky scope or architecture changes, stuck diagnostics, and acceptance handoff; skip deterministic one-step work unless an active lifecycle is at risk.
